Regression coverage defends both key precedence stories: outside a live Yazelix session, runtime-owned evidence beats stale ambient shell state; inside a live Yazelix session, the activated session profile still wins.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make materialized-state persistence use explicit recording helpers
description: Tighten persistent state ownership by replacing generic bootstrap-side mutation helpers with explicitly named recorders for materialized state and launch state. Today mark_config_state_applied and record_launch_state express two different ownership domains but still feel like generic state writes hanging off bootstrap/launch helpers. Refactor that surface so setup/install, launch/refresh, and live session activation each write only the state they actually own.
design: Clarify persistent-state ownership by naming the writes after the ownership domain they represent. The current split between mark_config_state_applied and record_launch_state is already better than before, but the mutation surface still hangs off generic bootstrap and launch helpers. Move toward explicit recorders for materialized state versus launch-state, and keep setup/install, refresh/build, and live launch flows from writing state they do not own.
acceptance_criteria: Persistent state writes are routed through explicitly named helpers that reflect ownership boundaries instead of generic bootstrap-side mutation. Setup or install flows only record the materialized state they own, while launch or refresh flows record launch-state/profile state they own. The resulting API makes it harder for future setup or shell-hook paths to overwrite launch-state accidentally.
close_reason: Completed

title: Deprecate generic get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR in favor of explicit root helpers
description: Follow through on the split-root architecture by retiring generic runtime aliases where explicit config, runtime, repo, state, or runtime-reference helpers now exist. Reduce use of get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR in code, tests, and docs so helpers stop hiding which ownership domain they actually mean. Keep compatibility only where it remains an intentional user-facing transition surface.
design: Treat this as the final cleanup slice after the explicit helper boundaries are in place. Replace get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR usages with config-root, runtime-root, state-root, repo-root, or runtime-reference helpers where the ownership domain is now known. Keep compatibility only at intentional external boundaries, and avoid turning this bead into a search-and-replace churn pass before the sharper helper APIs from the earlier children exist.
acceptance_criteria: Generic get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR usage is materially reduced in code, tests, and docs because call sites now use explicit root helpers that match the ownership domain they mean. Remaining compatibility uses are deliberate, documented, and narrow. New code in the affected areas no longer introduces fresh generic root alias usage by default.

title: Tighten duplicate refresh-state inspection between prelaunch and in-terminal startup
description: Yazelix no longer appears to do a true double rebuild for the recent desktop and launch paths, but there is still duplicated refresh-state inspection across the prelaunch path and the in-terminal startup path. Both sides reason about needs_refresh and related state before the real interactive session starts. Track the follow-up cleanup so the product keeps one real rebuild boundary while reducing duplicated state inspection and split-brain launch reasoning.
design: Keep this delete-first and narrow. The goal is not to merge every launch helper into one giant orchestrator. The goal is to stop both the external/prelaunch path and the in-terminal startup path from independently inspecting refresh state when only one side should own the authoritative rebuild decision. First map exactly which path should decide whether a rebuild is required and which path should simply consume the already-decided result. Then delete duplicate state checks, notices, and branching where they only restate the same decision. Preserve the current good property: one real rebuild boundary, visible when appropriate, without reintroducing hidden rebuilds or stale-session confusion.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is one clearly owned rebuild-decision boundary for the affected launch flow, rather than parallel prelaunch and in-terminal inspection of the same refresh state. 2. Remaining startup code consumes an explicit rebuild decision or artifact instead of recomputing the same launch-state judgment ad hoc. 3. The cleanup does not regress the visible-rebuild desktop contract, reuse mode, or terminal-wrapper repair logic. 4. Focused regressions defend that the flow still rebuilds exactly once when needed and does not silently hide rebuild work behind the launcher.

title: Test suite governance: keep every test relevant
description: Define how Yazelix decides whether a test belongs in the suite, which suite it belongs in, and when an existing test should be removed. The goal is to prevent the test surface from growing forever without clear relevance.
design: Start from first principles: every test should defend a real user-visible behavior, a regression that already happened, an integration boundary, a source-of-truth invariant, or an explicitly documented contract. This policy must apply to both new and existing tests. Define the actual Yazelix suite surfaces explicitly: the default yzx dev test path, optional heavier lanes such as sweep or visual integration checks, CI-only checks, and cheap local maintainer hooks like prek. Require a lightweight inventory of the current suites by test file or suite entrypoint, classify each bucket by the kind of contract it protects, and define what happens when a test cannot be tied to a living contract, regression, invariant, or accepted user-visible behavior: remove it, demote it out of the default suite, or explicitly grandfather and justify it. Prefer fewer higher-signal tests over broad shallow coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has a written test-policy document or issue design note that defines: 1. the real suite categories and command entrypoints (yzx dev test, optional heavier lanes, CI-only checks, and prek); 2. admission criteria for new tests; 3. retention and pruning rules for existing tests; and 4. when a test belongs in the default suite versus an optional lane. Existing tests are inventoried at least at the suite or file level against that policy, and at least one low-value or redundant test is removed, demoted, or explicitly grandfathered with justification. Future testing work can point to this policy when deciding whether a test is worth adding and where it belongs.
notes: Final shape: public test model simplified again after the aggressive prune. yzx dev test now runs the normal non-sweep automated regression suite with explicit membership: test_yzx_commands.nu, test_yzx_extra_regressions.nu, and test_reuse_mode.nu. The public --extended flag was removed because only a tiny number of extra checks remained and the split no longer earned its UX cost. Internal organization remains: core yzx contracts plus a small extra-regression bundle. The default suite currently covers 20 core checks + 6 extra regressions + 1 reuse-mode regression. yzx dev test --all now means default suite plus sweep + visual. Verified: validate_syntax, yzx dev test, direct core/extra/reuse entrypoints, validate_specs, and validate_readme_version all pass.
close_reason: Completed: added test-suite governance spec, switched to explicit default-suite membership, aggressively pruned low-value tests, and simplified the public yzx dev test model.

title: Investigate pane interactivity delay before prompt becomes usable
description: Imported GitHub issue #517. Investigate why opening a new pane can leave it unresponsive for a couple of seconds before Starship renders and typing works. Trace the exact pane-open path and identify whether the wait is in Zellij pane creation, shell bootstrap, devenv shell entry, prompt initialization, or another Yazelix-managed hook.
close_reason: Completed: pane interactivity delay fixed by trimming managed Nushell startup, routing new panes through the managed Yazelix Nushell wrapper, and defending the startup contracts with regressions.

title: Evaluate Ghostty package source policy
description: Evaluate which Ghostty package source Yazelix should prefer for its managed/default terminal path once the current integration bugs are fixed.
design: Compare the three realistic Ghostty package sources separately instead of treating 'Ghostty' as one blob: nixpkgs Ghostty on Linux, the upstream Ghostty flake, and system Ghostty / Homebrew on macOS. Judge them on stability, platform coverage, integration burden, update cadence, and whether they improve or complicate Yazelix's managed launch path. Do not let this policy work blur into the current Ghostty runtime/shader bug fixes.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear Ghostty package-source policy for Yazelix covering nixpkgs Ghostty, the upstream Ghostty flake, and system/Homebrew Ghostty on macOS. The chosen default(s) and any experimental/override path are explicit, with reasoning tied to platform support and maintenance cost.
notes: Decision on 2026-04-01: Yazelix should not switch its default managed Ghostty source from nixpkgs to the upstream Ghostty flake. The upstream flake is explicitly the tip/dev path, not the regular-user package path, and it carries the same non-NixOS OpenGL caveat. Recommended policy shape: macOS should continue to use system/Homebrew Ghostty; NixOS can continue to treat nixpkgs Ghostty as the normal managed package path; non-NixOS Linux should stop assuming nixpkgs Ghostty is a friction-free default and should likely prefer another default terminal or a system Ghostty path unless the user explicitly opts into the Nix-managed Ghostty path and its GPU/driver caveats.
close_reason: Completed

title: Managed popup pane lifecycle: toggle, focus, reuse, no duplicates
description: Move transient popup-pane lifecycle into a dedicated popup runner plus a small shell toggle wrapper so popup actions behave like managed session state rather than disposable launches. The popup key should toggle a single managed popup pane: open it if missing, focus it if present but unfocused, and close it if focused. Repeated presses must not spawn duplicate floating panes.
design: Keep this scoped to one managed popup identity first, backed by the existing configurable popup_program. Use the shell-side yzx popup path for creation because it is the proven reliable floating-pane launcher. Let the popup runner own discovery, focus, close, and reuse semantics so shell glue does not have to infer floating-pane state from raw Zellij output. Preserve the current configurable popup command surface, but make the lifecycle stateful and session-aware without prematurely generalizing to multiple popup types.
acceptance_criteria:
Pressing the popup key opens the configured popup program in one managed floating pane when none exists.
Pressing it again focuses the existing popup if it exists but is not focused, and closes it if it is focused.
Repeated presses do not create duplicate floating panes.
Add regression coverage at the popup-runner/plugin seam for popup discovery and toggle behavior.
Add an end-to-end Yazelix script test that verifies no duplicate popup panes are created across repeated toggles.
close_reason: Implemented managed popup lifecycle with hybrid shell/plugin toggle flow

title: add yzx import zellij/yazi to import native configs into yazelix-managed overrides
description: Add a short import flow that brings native Zellij and Yazi config files under Yazelix management instead of making users manually copy files into the managed override paths.
design:
Keep the command surface short and product-shaped: use `yzx import zellij` and `yzx import yazi` instead of a generic `yzx cp` command or a 4-word `yzx config import ...` flow. Treat this as an adoption/import path, not a reset path: the source is the native config location and the destination is Yazelix's managed override location.

For Zellij, import only `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/zellij/config.kdl`. For Yazi, import only the override files Yazelix already has a clear contract for: `yazi.toml`, `keymap.toml`, and `init.lua` from `~/.config/yazi/` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazi/`. Do not try to import plugin directories, flavors, caches, or other broader state in v1.

Fail fast when the managed destination already exists so users do not silently clobber their Yazelix-managed files. Allow an explicit `--force` path, but back up any overwritten managed file first and keep the messaging clear about what was imported, skipped, backed up, or overwritten.
acceptance_criteria: `yzx import zellij` imports `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/zellij/config.kdl`. `yzx import yazi` imports `yazi.toml`, `keymap.toml`, and `init.lua` from `~/.config/yazi/` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazi/`. Missing native source files fail clearly instead of silently succeeding. Existing managed destination files are not overwritten unless `--force` is passed, and the `--force` path writes backups before replacing managed files. The docs point users to the import commands as the supported way to adopt native Zellij or Yazi config into Yazelix-managed overrides. Add focused command-level regression coverage for the happy path and overwrite protection.
close_reason: Completed

title: Migrate legacy [packs] out of yazelix.toml and make legacy pack placement fail fast
close_reason: Completed

title: Extract a backend adapter boundary around env, run, refresh, and launch bootstrap
description: Refactor the command/bootstrap layer so the current devenv integration sits behind a clearer backend adapter boundary instead of bleeding directly into user-facing command semantics. Focus on the shared paths now rooted in environment_bootstrap.nu and the yzx env/run/refresh/launch surface. The immediate goal is not to support a second backend in main; it is to make current devenv-specific assumptions explicit, shrink accidental coupling, and leave behind a clearer seam for later experiments or a Core edition.
design: Treat this as main-branch architecture cleanup, not speculative abstraction. The important move is to identify what the command layer expects from the environment backend and to keep the rest of the command semantics outside that adapter. Likely hot spots include get_devenv_base_command, run_in_devenv_shell_command, resolve_preferred_devenv_path, and the launch/bootstrap state that currently assumes devenv-shaped profiles. Delete direct ad hoc devenv assumptions where possible, but do not build a pretend generic backend framework unless the resulting seam is materially clearer. This should complement ongoing env-var cleanup in yazelix-2ex.2.1 rather than duplicating it.
acceptance_criteria: The env/run/refresh/launch bootstrap path has a clearer backend boundary, with fewer direct devenv-specific assumptions bleeding into user-facing command semantics. The repo shape is simpler to reason about than before, not more abstract. Future backend experiments or a Core edition would have a concrete seam to target instead of rediscovering environment_bootstrap.nu as a monolith.

title: Model launch, refresh, and profile reuse as an explicit runtime state machine
description: Make the runtime-control plane explicit instead of continuing to spread launch, refresh, reuse, and activation decisions across bootstrap helpers and command entrypoints. The goal is a single state model for when Yazelix can reuse a built profile, when it must rebuild, and how live activation state differs from persisted launch truth.
design: Treat this as a product-state-model bead, not just a refactor. Enumerate the meaningful runtime states and transitions first, then make yzx launch, enter, env, run, refresh, and restart consume that model instead of hand-rolling overlapping checks. The design should make rebuild-vs-reuse-vs-force-reenter decisions legible and should keep the distinction between materialized state and stale ambient shell activation explicit. Decide whether the resulting state engine stays in Nushell or deserves a smaller compiled supervisor, but make the transition model explicit either way.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The runtime launch model has explicit states and transitions for rebuild, reuse, refresh, force-reenter, and live activation. 2. The main user-facing entrypoints share one transition model instead of re-encoding should-refresh/profile-selection logic independently. 3. The model preserves the current runtime/config/state ownership contract. 4. Follow-up implementation work can target explicit transitions instead of scattered shell-condition cleanup.

title: Add yzx dev lint_nu maintainer command
description: Add a lint_nu subcommand to yzx dev that runs nu-lint with the repo .nu-lint.toml config against the nushell/ directory. Should use yzx run for tool invocation.
close_reason: Added yzx dev lint_nu command to dev.nu. Supports --format (pretty/compact) and optional path arguments. Checks for nu-lint availability and .nu-lint.toml presence with clear errors.

title: Evaluate Lenia welcome style later
design: Defer Lenia until the welcome-style system exists and the cost/readability tradeoffs are proven. Prefer a pre-rendered or precomputed frame-sequence evaluation first; only consider live Lenia later if it proves clearly worth the complexity. This bead is for evaluating whether Lenia should join the animated pool later, not for forcing a live simulation into startup prematurely.
acceptance_criteria: The evaluation documents whether Lenia should be live, precomputed, or rejected for startup use, and only proceeds after the shared welcome_style selector exists.

title: Profile desktop and managed-terminal startup as first-class scenarios
description:
yazelix-3n1 upgraded the current-terminal profiling surface, but desktop launch and managed new-window launch remain distinct user-felt startup paths with their own prelaunch logic, wrapper repair behavior, and terminal-selection contracts. They need first-class profiling scenarios instead of being inferred from the enter path.

Design:
Extend the profiling surface carefully instead of creating a second profiler. Add explicit scenario coverage for desktop launch and managed new-window launch so reports can attribute time to prelaunch work, visible bootstrap paths, terminal-wrapper repair, and the transition into the actual Yazelix session. Keep the same structured report schema and avoid speculative instrumentation that does not correspond to real ownership boundaries.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The profiling surface can produce structured reports for desktop or managed new-window startup, not only current-terminal entry. 2. The measured phases include any prelaunch work that users actually wait on in those flows. 3. Reports stay comparable with the existing current-terminal profiling schema. 4. Documentation explains which scenarios are covered and how to invoke them.

title: Fail fast for yzx dev commands when no writable repo checkout is available
description:
Maintainer commands such as ⚠️  This updates Yazelix maintainer inputs to latest upstream versions.
   The hardened flow updates devenv.lock locally, then runs canary refresh/build checks before finishing.
   Broken updates should stay local and never be pushed.
Continue? [y/N]: Aborted. should not drift into runtime-store paths or fail deep inside devenv 2.0.7 (x86_64-linux) when no writable Yazelix checkout is available. Make the maintainer surface fail fast with a clear repo-root requirement and a short recovery hint whenever it is invoked outside a usable checkout context.
close_reason: Added Alt+1..9 documentation to yzx keys output in keys.nu

title: Preserve managed panes across Alt y / Alt [ ] layout transitions
description: GitHub #490: layout changes driven by the pane orchestrator can replace the live editor pane with a generic shell pane because override_layout currently rebuilds anonymous terminal slots instead of matching retained panes by exact run instruction. Scope covers Alt y, Alt [, and Alt ] because they all go through the same override_layout path.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Audit the yzx command surface into backend-required versus backend-agnostic commands
description: Inventory the yzx command surface and classify which commands fundamentally require a managed environment backend and which commands belong to the backend-agnostic UX/runtime layer. This should cover launch, edit, doctor, desktop install, config migration, update flows, env/run/refresh, and any commands that only read or generate Yazelix-owned state. The result should make it much easier to reason about current product boundaries and a future Core edition without guessing command by command.
close_reason: Duplicate of yazelix-d4pw created during parallel bead creation.

title: Evaluate centralizing more shared data into constants.nu and whether it now needs refactoring
description: Audit the current role of nushell/scripts/utils/constants.nu and decide whether more shared data should be centralized there or whether the file has reached the point where further growth would be counterproductive. Scope: inventory existing constants-like data that is still duplicated elsewhere, identify what truly benefits from centralization, and recommend whether constants.nu should stay as-is, absorb more shared data, or be split into smaller focused modules.
design: Treat this as a focused audit, not as a mandate to turn constants.nu into a dumping ground. Identify which duplicated values are genuinely constants-like and shared across multiple real consumers, and which ones should stay near the subsystem that owns them. The output should distinguish between centralizing more data into constants.nu, leaving the file mostly as-is, or splitting it into smaller focused modules if its current role has become too mixed.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete recommendation for constants.nu: keep as-is, centralize a specific shortlist of additional shared data, or refactor the file into smaller modules. The recommendation is grounded in actual duplicated data ownership sites, not taste alone. Any suggested next steps leave the repo with clearer ownership of shared values, not more indirection.

title: Fix Alt-( and Alt-) keybindings in Helix under Zellij
description: Fix the Alt-( and Alt-) Helix keybindings when running under Zellij so these editor shortcuts behave reliably instead of getting lost or mangled by the terminal and Zellij layer.
design: Treat this as an input-stack bug spanning terminal, Zellij, and Helix. First confirm what escape sequences are emitted in the supported terminal set, then decide whether the fix belongs in Zellij keybindings, terminal keyboard protocol settings, Helix mappings, or some narrower compatibility layer. Avoid a fix that only works in one terminal if Yazelix claims broader support.
acceptance_criteria: The affected Alt bindings work in the supported Yazelix terminal stack or the docs state the exact caveat when they cannot. Add unit tests for escape-sequence normalization or mapping helpers where they exist, regression tests or scripted input capture, and end-to-end validation with detailed logs of the emitted sequences and the final behavior inside Helix.
close_reason: Closed after fresh manual verification confirmed Alt-( and Alt-) currently work in the supported Yazelix stack.

title: Restore Starship Yazi plugin prompt integration
description: GitHub issue #529 reports that the Starship Yazi plugin has regressed and Yazi is showing the normal prompt instead of the plugin-driven one. Investigate the current Yazi shell/prompt wiring, identify where the plugin path stopped taking effect, and restore the intended prompt integration without depending on host-local prompt setup.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Popup command should respect editor.command
description: GitHub #513: yzx_popup flows such as jjui editing a commit description should respect the configured editor.command instead of falling back to the wrong editor binary.

title: Popup pane open latency is too high
description: Track the managed popup path taking around five seconds to open while closing remains fast. This is a popup-open-path performance bug, not a generic pane-slow issue. The work should identify where the latency actually lives: popup launch orchestration, Zellij pane creation, plugin coordination, shell/env setup, or the launched popup program itself, then remove unnecessary work or cache the expensive step.
design: Treat this as a measurement-first bug. Reproduce the latency with a concrete popup command and instrument the open path from /Alt+t through pane creation to first usable frame. Keep the scope on open latency only; close is already fast and should remain unchanged. Prefer deleting work or reusing existing state over adding more control layers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is a reproducible measurement of popup open latency before and after the fix. 2. The main source of open-path cost is identified and documented. 3. The managed popup opens substantially faster in normal use without regressing close/focus/toggle behavior. 4. Add one focused regression or benchmark guard at the seam that actually caused the latency.
close_reason: Popup wrappers now avoid importing the full core command suite on popup open, removing the main measured bootstrap cost.

title: Audit refresh and restart flows against cached launch state
description: Verify the real effects of yzx refresh and yzx restart against the cached launch profile, generated configs, and runtime state. Catch cases where Yazelix reports success but still reuses an environment that lacks the newly configured terminal or other runtime changes.
close_reason: Audit completed: launch and restart now reject stale cached/current environments that lack the configured terminal and re-enter a fresh environment.

title: ability to derive a yazelix.toml + yazelix_packs.toml into a flake

Imported GitHub issue #548.

ability to flakify the config and use the flake without devenv

title: Make adding custom Zellij layouts straightforward
description: Imported GitHub issue #438 after verifying that custom Zellij layouts are only easy in a narrow no-sidebar case and are otherwise blocked by hardcoded layout generation, hardcoded sidebar-family knowledge in the pane orchestrator, and stale docs.
design: Treat this as a structural customization problem rather than a docs-only paper cut. The supported path for adding layouts should be explicit: layout files must be discoverable or registered through one clear mechanism, sidebar layout families must expose enough metadata for the pane orchestrator to switch and toggle them without hardcoded family names, and unsupported layouts must fail fast with clear errors instead of silently disappearing. The documentation should reflect the actual current filenames and the real extension workflow.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can add a supported custom layout without touching multiple unrelated code paths by hand; generated Zellij config picks up the new layout through the documented mechanism; sidebar-aware layouts either work through a documented manifest or naming scheme or fail fast with a clear error; docs describe the current layout files and the actual extension workflow; automated tests cover the supported custom-layout path.
close_reason: Made top-level Zellij layout discovery automatic, documented the supported customization boundary, and added regression coverage for custom layout generation.

title: Use a floating Yazi picker from Helix when the sidebar is disabled
description: Narrower slice of the broader floating Yazi picker feature: provide a picker flow that remains useful when the normal sidebar is disabled, likely using a Zellij floating pane instead of depending on the persistent side surface.
design: Keep this aligned with the broader floating-picker direction instead of creating a second unrelated Helix-Yazi picker implementation. The key question is how the picker should behave when the file-tree sidebar is unavailable: invocation path, floating-pane UX, selected-path return, and cancellation behavior. This should remain compatible with a future general floating picker from Helix.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear design or implementation path for using a floating Yazi picker from Helix when the sidebar is disabled, and it remains aligned with the broader picker feature rather than diverging into a duplicate flow.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Make Helix reveal integration self-contained in Yazelix

The goal is for Yazelix-managed Helix sessions to get the reveal binding and related Yazelix-specific config from Yazelix-owned state instead of depending on the user's personal ~/.config/helix/config.toml.

title: Desktop relaunch rebuild must stay visible instead of happening invisibly
description: After the runtime-identity hash churn fix, desktop relaunch no longer always forces stale rebuilds, but when a rebuild is genuinely needed it can still happen before the terminal window becomes visible again. Track the remaining desktop-entry UX regression so rebuild/setup work happens in the visible Yazelix window rather than invisibly behind the launcher.

title: Add boids welcome style
design: Add a boids-inspired welcome style that stays cheap enough for startup, runs for a short bounded time, and resolves into a readable final frame. The implementation may be deterministic lightweight live rendering or pre-rendered frame playback, whichever better preserves the startup budget and layout fit.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting welcome_style = boids renders the boids sequence within the startup budget, preserves readability in narrow layouts, and is defended by tests for bounded frame generation and layout fit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make the installed runtime own devenv so yzx update runtime can subsume yzx update devenv
description: Today the installed runtime still depends on a separate profile-installed devenv CLI. Runtime-localize devenv so the flake-installed Yazelix runtime carries the exact devenv surface it needs, bootstrap prefers that runtime-local path, and the user-facing update model can collapse toward one runtime update instead of separate runtime and devenv update commands.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not add another compatibility layer around the existing split if the real fix is to stop depending on a separately managed profile CLI. First prove the runtime can ship or otherwise deterministically resolve the exact devenv it needs. Then switch bootstrap and launch helpers to prefer that runtime-local devenv path. Only after that should Yazelix reconsider whether yzx update devenv still needs to exist as a first-class user command.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The installed/flaked Yazelix runtime no longer requires a separate user-profile devenv install for normal launch, refresh, restart, and desktop-launch flows. 2. Bootstrap and launch helpers resolve a runtime-local or otherwise runtime-owned devenv path first. 3. The user-facing update contract is reevaluated afterward, with a clear keep/remove decision for yzx update devenv. 4. The result simplifies, rather than multiplies, update surfaces and bootstrap state.
close_reason: Completed

title: for every comment that is automated or similar prefix it with something like "Automated: <message>"

could be only for new comments

title: Revisit override-layout via owned explicit-run pane creation
description: Capture the future path for returning to Zellij override-layout transitions after the current rollback to layout-family swapping. The experiment showed that override_layout only becomes viable if Yazelix owns the relevant pane-creation surfaces and opens panes with stable explicit run instructions instead of default-shell panes that Zellij normalizes to invoked_with = None. Scope this as design + implementation exploration, not an immediate bugfix. Evaluate which pane creation surfaces Yazelix can realistically own (Alt+m, pane-mode creation, split flows), whether built-in Zellij Run/NewTiledPane/NewStackedPane actions are enough, and what exact contract would let Alt y / Alt [ ] preserve live panes under override_layout without duplicating panes or sidebar state.
design: Treat this as the current explicit-run path back toward override-layout after the earlier rollback. The core question is whether Yazelix can own enough pane-creation surfaces to preserve live panes under override_layout by using stable explicit run instructions instead of anonymous shell panes. This should remain a design and contract bead first, not a promise that override-layout is immediately ready to ship again.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete contract for what Yazelix would need to own in order to make override-layout viable again, including pane creation surfaces, run-instruction identity, and layout-transition invariants. If the path is still too costly or constrained, that is captured explicitly instead of leaving override-layout in limbo.

title: Add lightweight profiling for yzx test suites
