title: Ghostty trail glow "none" still renders extreme glow
description:
Imported GitHub issue #466.

### Details

When glow is set to `none`, Yazelix still renders a very large spatial glow instead of disabling the effect.
close_reason: Completed: fixed Ghostty cursor glow mask math and normalized trail glow behavior so none/low/medium/high behave correctly across variants.

title: Evaluate Pretext-inspired text-first welcome screen
description: Use the Pretext site as inspiration for another strong Yazelix welcome-screen direction, focused on text-first composition rather than only boxed logo cards.
design:
Use the Pretext site as inspiration for a more text-forward Yazelix welcome treatment, but test the palette and copy hierarchy step by step instead of composing multiple experimental color systems at once.

Color findings from the shipped logo asset (assets/logo.png): the brand palette is red, green, yellow, blue, and purple. In practice, purple and red should stay out of readable body copy; they work better as wordmark or border accents. The next controlled trials should compare:
- Brand Calm: blue body base with yellow highlights and green only for special emphasis like SSH
- Matrix Clean: green body base with restrained yellow highlights

Preferred first trial: Matrix Clean, because it fits the terminal-native identity and the user explicitly likes that direction. Keep the wordmark multicolor, keep the border single-color, and keep the body text color system simple: one base color plus one accent color. Avoid alternating full-line palettes and avoid more than one or two highlighted terms per line.

This bead is specifically about the text card composition and palette, not about adding another animation engine.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented, repo-backed color direction for the text-first welcome card that is derived from the shipped logo rather than ad hoc terminal guesses. At least one restrained palette variant is tested end to end on the real welcome card, with width-fit checks, readability checks on the actual background, and a clear keep/change decision before moving on to more experimentation. The implementation should preserve the existing welcome_style system and should not add extra color complexity unless a previous simpler variant has been evaluated and rejected.

title: Verify all Yazelix update and refresh commands against real installed/runtime flows
description: Audit and verify the real behavior of Yazelix update-related commands such as install, refresh, restart, desktop install, and update devenv. The goal is to catch cases where commands appear successful but leave stale runtime, hook, desktop, or generated-config state behind.
design: Treat update-related commands as a product system, not isolated commands. Verify the actual end-to-end effects of install, refresh, restart, desktop install, and update devenv against the real installed runtime, generated configs, shell hooks, desktop entry, and cached launch profile. The point is to catch stale-state failures where commands report success but leave the user on an old runtime, old desktop entry, old hook path, or mismatched generated config.
acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit audit of the supported update and refresh commands, with the real state each command is supposed to update. Commands that leave stale or misleading state have either been fixed or spun into concrete follow-up bugs. The result should make it much harder for Yazelix to report success while the user is still running an old runtime, old desktop asset, or stale generated config.
close_reason: Completed the end-to-end update audit for install, desktop install, refresh, restart, update devenv, and dev update; fixed the stale-shell, canary, and packaged update-surface bugs and spun remaining UX polish into follow-up beads.

title: Narrow backend comparison issue into a Pixi-first exploration lane
description: GitHub issue #518 asked for a broad backend comparison across devenv, mise, brioche, pixi, and prefix. That framing mixed tools from different layers. The outcome is to close the broad issue and keep the real exploration scoped to a Pixi-first experiment branch instead of a generic multi-backend bakeoff.
close_reason: Closed in favor of the narrower Pixi-first evaluation lane tracked separately in yazelix-qow.

title: Auto-apply safe config migrations at entrypoints while keeping yzx config migrate
description:
Imported GitHub issue #478.

Yazelix should automatically apply deterministic config migrations when the user enters the product through the normal interactive entrypoints, instead of forcing them to manually run `yzx config migrate --apply` for every stale-but-safe case. This should make startup, launch, restart, and interactive `yzx env` feel self-healing for safe rewrites while preserving explicit control and clear diagnostics for ambiguous cases.

Do not remove `yzx config migrate`. It should remain the canonical preview/apply/debug surface for migrations. The change here is to make the common entrypoints invoke the same safe migration engine automatically when that is clearly safe.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Deterministic rewrites with backups are product-maintenance work, not user work, so Yazelix should usually handle them automatically when the user is trying to launch or enter the environment.
- Read paths must stay read-only. Do not hide file mutations inside `parse_yazelix_config` or other helpers used by widgets, config generation, or general state inspection. Ordinary reads should not rewrite user config.
- Keep the existing split between safe `auto` migrations and `manual_only` migrations. Automatic entrypoint behavior must only apply the `auto` subset and must leave manual-only items explicit.

Scope:
- Add a migration preflight for interactive entrypoints such as `yzx launch`, `yzx restart`, normal Yazelix startup, and interactive `yzx env`.
- If the migration plan has only deterministic auto rewrites, apply them automatically with backups, then continue into the normal entrypoint flow and print a short high-signal notice.
- If the plan has both auto and manual-only items, apply only the auto rewrites, then continue while printing the remaining manual follow-up clearly.
- If the situation is ambiguous or ownership-sensitive, keep failing fast with the current actionable diagnostics instead of guessing.
- In non-interactive or read-only contexts, do not mutate automatically.

Non-goals:
- Do not remove `yzx config migrate`.
- Do not make `parse_yazelix_config` mutate files.
- Do not auto-apply `manual_only` migrations.
- Do not silently resolve duplicate or conflicting ownership cases that still require user intent.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Interactive entrypoints (`yzx launch`, `yzx restart`, normal Yazelix startup, and interactive `yzx env`) run a migration preflight before normal config consumption.
2. When the migration plan contains only `auto` changes, Yazelix applies them automatically with backup and continues into the entrypoint flow without requiring the user to run `yzx config migrate --apply` manually.
3. When the plan contains both `auto` and `manual_only` items, Yazelix applies only the `auto` subset, surfaces the remaining manual items clearly, and still fails fast when those manual-only items require user intent before startup can continue.
4. When the config state is ambiguous or ownership-sensitive, Yazelix still fails fast with clear guidance instead of auto-mutating.
5. Non-interactive/read-only paths such as widget rendering, general config parsing helpers, and other routine readers do not mutate config files.
6. `yzx config migrate` remains available as the explicit preview/apply/debug surface and still reports the same plan semantics.
7. Add focused regression coverage for: auto-apply on at least one interactive entrypoint, mixed auto/manual plans, non-interactive no-mutation behavior, and continued fail-fast handling for ambiguous ownership conflicts.
close_reason: Completed: interactive entrypoints now auto-apply deterministic config migrations with backups, still fail fast on remaining manual-only follow-up, and keep parse/read paths non-mutating.

title: Remove tracked .vscode repo state
description: Remove the tracked .vscode/settings.json Kiro-local preference, ignore .vscode/, and keep editor-local repo settings out of git by default.
close_reason: Resolved earlier: .vscode is ignored and no .vscode files are tracked.

title: Re-verify Alt y does not re-enable pane frames zellij-wide
description: GitHub issue #484 reports that pressing Alt y makes pane frames appear again across the Zellij session and reintroduces the shaky pane-frame bug. Current code history includes a fix removing the old frame-toggle hook, but this needs live behavioral verification before the issue can be closed.
close_reason: Fixed the Alt+y pane-frame regression; the remaining sidebar-reopen bug is tracked separately.

title: Fix leaked __YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR__ placeholder in Zoxide editor launcher
description: Opening a file from the Zoxide editor flow currently fails because the generated command still contains the literal __YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR__ placeholder instead of a real runtime path. The failure shows up as a command like 'nu __YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR__/nushell/scripts/integrations/zoxide_open_in_editor.nu ...', so the editor pane never opens and the runtime contract is broken.
design: Treat this as a runtime-generation and command-templating bug, not a user-local recovery. Identify where the Zoxide editor command is supposed to resolve the runtime path, remove any duplicate placeholder plumbing that can leave __YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR__ unsubstituted, and make the launcher consume one canonical runtime-path source. If substitution is missing, fail fast with a clear runtime-path error instead of emitting a broken command string into the live session.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The Zoxide editor flow never emits a literal __YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR__ path in the launched command. 2. Opening a file from the Zoxide/Yazi editor surface works in a normal managed Yazelix session. 3. The runtime path used by this launcher comes from one explicit canonical source instead of ad hoc placeholder substitution. 4. A focused regression defends the seam that previously emitted the broken command.

title: Stop exporting source-checkout runtime identity through ambient YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR
description: Delete the source-shell export of YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR from devenv enterShell so repo-loaded code and generated wrappers stop inheriting the installed runtime by accident. Keep explicit runtime env only where cross-process handoff genuinely needs it, and defend the source-checkout launch path against a conflicting installed-runtime env. Split shellHook runtime identity so repo-local shells keep DEVENV_ROOT as the live source surface while internal setup and config-state bookkeeping can still use an explicit runtime root when needed.

title: Evaluate whether yzx cwd should be removed, narrowed, or kept
description: Evaluate whether the yzx cwd command still deserves to exist as a first-class command surface, and if so what narrower contract it should have.
design: Reason from user-facing intent rather than implementation habit. Determine what real job yzx cwd is doing today: tab naming, workspace retargeting, cwd synchronization, or a compatibility shim for older flows. Then compare that against overlapping surfaces and recent bugs. If the command is confusing or too easy to misuse, prefer narrowing or deleting it with a clear migration story rather than preserving it out of inertia.
acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit decision to keep, narrow, rename, or remove yzx cwd. The bead records the user-facing contract if it survives, or the replacement and migration story if it does not. Any decision accounts for overlaps with newer workspace and tab-cwd flows.

title: Manually validate Yazelix installation and startup through Home Manager for the first time
description: Run a real first-time Home Manager install of Yazelix on a clean user setup, verify runtime/current ownership, desktop entry, yzx shim, launch flow, and managed config generation, then record any gaps between the documented Home Manager path and actual user experience.
design: Treat this as a real user-path validation bead, not as another flake-check proxy. The important thing is to exercise the documented Home Manager install story from a clean user perspective and record where setup, runtime ownership, generated configs, desktop entry integration, or launch behavior diverge from the intended contract. Keep the validation focused on the canonical Home Manager path rather than maintainer-only local shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: There is a manual validation record for the Home Manager install/startup path covering first-time installation, runtime/current ownership, yzx wrapper behavior, generated config presence, desktop entry path, and successful launch. Any gaps found are either fixed or split into explicit follow-up beads, leaving the Home Manager story better defined than before.
notes:
Validation on 2026-04-08:
- Lived-in Home Manager takeover now validates runtime/current, ~/.local/bin/yzx, managed TOML surfaces, and doctor behavior.
- Fixed Home Manager symlinked config-surface handling and Home Manager-aware doctor/install diagnostics.
- Verified Home Manager-managed yzx shim shapes and profile desktop-entry path.
- Split follow-up bugs instead of overloading this bead: yazelix-iu52 (clean-room host-dotfile assumption), yazelix-r5ca (clean-room refresh missing generated Zellij layout), and yazelix-isdn (manual-install takeover still collides on ~/.local/bin/yzx).
- Longer-term takeover helper is tracked separately in yazelix-x0v2.2; this bead still owns the documented Home Manager migration and validation story, including whether the recommended home-manager switch path and backup guidance are actually good enough.
- Bead stays in progress until the remaining clean-room startup blockers are resolved or explicitly re-scoped.

title: Make the Yazelix desktop entry stable across runtime updates
description: The desktop entry should not keep going stale whenever the installed runtime store path changes. Replace the current runtime-targeted desktop-entry contract with a stable launcher path so normal installs and updates do not require rerunning yzx desktop install just to keep the desktop entry working.
design: Treat the current stale-desktop-entry detection as a guardrail, not the final UX. The real contract problem is that the desktop file points at a specific runtime store path, so normal runtime updates can invalidate it. Replace that with a stable launcher surface such as ~/.local/bin/yzx or ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/current so desktop launches remain valid across installs and updates. Preserve clear diagnostics, but the goal is to stop needing repeated yzx desktop install repairs in normal use.
acceptance_criteria: A previously installed Yazelix desktop entry continues to work after normal runtime updates without needing yzx desktop install again. The desktop entry uses a stable launcher path instead of a specific runtime store path. Doctor and launch-time checks may still detect genuinely broken installs, but normal runtime churn should not make the desktop entry stale.
close_reason: Completed
